review_title: Auvik is amazing
description: best Monitoring tool I have ever used. We currently relaying on Auvik alerts as our main monitoring source, I have been using it for almost two years, but I will never think there will be better tool for same purpose.
author: Hemin K.
pros: Easy of managing devices, the user interface is cool as you can have visibility to your network in best way. Great monitoring system, alert management, great customer support, one of the best remote access tools I have ever used especially for MSPs, as it makes very easy to add/ manage different sites.
cons: cli integration needs more improvement, the web interface for CLI doesn't support all required feature for network engineers

review_title: Auvik for Everyone!
description: Auvik simplifies the IT work cycle from monitoring, troubleshooting, to administration. It presents both high-level correlation and granular root causes in a single management plane. Allowing optimized decision making for both management and administrators.
author: Engels P.
pros: The correlation between events, alerts, and granularity of reports. The customer support is top-tier and speaks to the commitment and understanding of the client-base.
cons: Missing automation, and CVE patching. With the partner-level information about EOL/EOS devices, automation of remediation is the next-level frontier missing.

review_title: Powerful and reliable network monitoring for MSPs
description: The overall experience is good once the product is well configured at a client, it also integrates well with Connectwise RMM
author: Philippe C.
pros: Auvik gives a quick overall view of the network. It's great for fast troubleshooting and insight. Also, the network device config backups can be a life-saver in critical situations.
cons: We found that support can sometime be a little slow to respond when correcting bugs liked to the GUI.

review_title: MSP NOC Resources Real Review
description: As a help desk manager/resource who has to work after hours NOC alerts just getting to the correct place in Auvik can be hard but the feature set is amazing. It has everything we need as a small company to make sure our customers stay online.
author: Harrison K.
pros: I like how Auvik can just find all network devices and suggest what they are/what they do before I tell it.
cons: The complicated interface can be hard to learn when coming from another platform. It was complicated for me personally never using a platform quite like Auvik.

review_title: “Powerful Network Monitoring with Great Visibility”
description: Overall, Auvik has given us visibility into our network that we didn’t have before. It’s helped reduce downtime, streamline troubleshooting, and improve security posture. Auvik has become an indispensable part of our daily IT operations. It saves time, reduces headaches, and gives peace of mind knowing the network is being actively monitored.
author: Russell P.
pros: The intuitive interface makes it easy to quickly see the health of the entire network. The monitoring and alerting capabilities are excellent, and the remote access features save a ton of time troubleshooting without having to roll a truck. Reporting is clear and helps justify decisions with leadership. Customer support has been responsive and knowledgeable whenever I’ve needed them.
cons: The learning curve can be a little steep at first, especially when digging into more advanced configuration and reporting. Some of the licensing tiers can feel expensive for smaller environments. I’d also like to see a bit more flexibility in customizing dashboards.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Auvik: The best & easiest choice!
description: Their support, and onboarding was wonderful. They answered all of my questions, helped us through understanding all of our logs and where to configure our devices. Auvik made it delightful to integrate into our infrastructure. I will continue using their product in the future and am now a massive advocate for it.
author: Josh H.
pros: Our low headcount for our team makes it so that we can be spread thin from our projects and constant end user needs. We needed a monitoring solution that was something we could stand up quickly and that had all the features needed to troubleshoot quickly through our network. Standing it up so quickly while having the depth we needed was our absolute favorite!
cons: I thought the actual dashboards to be able to display on our TV's was lacking. However, this is a feature they are currently and actively working on and so I feel this is just a temporary con, and know it will get incredibly better on this!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy to use network monitoring system
description: After using Auvik for a couple of years, I find it to be really useful for alerts which are easily customised and paused. We have scheduled a pause for some of or APs going offline over night when we have scheduled them to power down via the switch.
author: Pete E.
pros: It is incredibly simple to use and quick to navigate around.
cons: Trying to locate where a data spike is originating from is tricky.

review_title: Auvik from a Network Engineers perspective.
description: Overall, I am very pleased and happy with Auvik. I get those adds for Auvik saying sign up for a webinar and get a free Ubiquiti Switch and think dang, I wish I could sign up for that again! I never got a cool toy to play with, just a really helpful tool. In all seriousness If you are shopping for a tool that will assist your Team with managing, Alerting, and creating a Standard tool for troubleshooting then Auvik is hands down a solid product I respect and would recommend. Auvik and 1Password are really the only two companies I even care enough to write a review for if that says anything at all.
author: David B.
pros: One of the Biggest Pros for me is that Auvik is a tool that's simple yet powerful and is very easy to get going. It's not like other tools where you need to go through a month of training to understand it. As a MSP Network Engineer Auvik has helped me gain a better insight to my environments. Its lightweight deployment and has plenty of awesome features like the remote access tool which I love.
cons: It has its issues especially when it comes to the customization of Alerting. That being said there is good news, Auvik recently launched Alerting 2.0 which allows much more customization. Hopefully they will release the predefined alerts in 2.0 versioning. Support has been easy to work with and while some times they are not always the most prompt they do remember to follow up as well as notify you.

review_title: Auvik is amazing!
description: Overwhelmingly positive! Besides the amazing product and services, the staff is amazing. I can honestly say that I've never dealt with a nicer customer service team. And even above that was the sales/onboarding team. Just a great experience.
author: Mark S.
pros: Auvik's dashboard is an immensely helpful space to get a quick status of my network. My favorite feature is the built-in remote access to my printers and switches. Of course the best overall feature is the timely and highly customizable alert setup.
cons: I wouldn't say this is a con so much, but for me, the network map export options are a little limited. I prefer to work with native Window apps for security reasons, but I had to link up with Lucid to get a good and useable view of my map. This could likely be an issue with my understanding of the process though.

review_title: Great tool for large network evironments
description: I'm extremely satisfied with Auvik, it is a very useful tool. I would highly recommend this product to other Technology evironments.
author: Greg S.
pros: Alerting system on critical devices within the organization allowing our company to fix the issues prior to it become a major problem
cons: Some of the network mapping bleeds into other networks thus not giving a correct picture of that network

review_title: Best NMS on the market for MSPs
description: Overall, it has been extremely positive. I have used Auvik for 5+ years and don't think I will ever want to use an alternate solution.
author: Lorenzo K.
pros: Overall network visibility and a myriad of configuration options and features. I know what i am seeing in Auvik is accurate and reliable. Remote management of things like switches is incredible. Very MSP Friendly!
cons: UI can be intimidating for new users, and lacks customization.

review_title: I like Auvik
description: It's an amazing tool that helps you to track the work of all the members of the company, is super helpful for companies that work with people in other states or even contries.
author: Marian V.
pros: It's super useful for companies that work remote.
cons: It's not easy to use at first, but you can learn.

review_title: Great for network overview
author: Colin B.
pros: The network map was extremely useful, and being able to search all the devices on the network by category came in handy quite a bit.
cons: Sometimes it could be a little slow to load in certain browsers, but it wasn't a major issue at all.

review_title: Helps Provide Solid Network Management Functions for an MSP
author: Rick R.
pros: The software is subscription based and only requires that a collector service be installed and running on a windows machine in the network to manage. This makes deployment quite easy and quick to put in motion. The discovery function is pretty straightforward and begins building an inventory and topology right away. Once you get managed devices set up with SNMP and login credentials, the topology starts to shape up and configurations start getting backed up. We integrate this with our PSA system and the ability to do bi-directional ticket creation and closure works quite well. This is a solid product for MSPs and makes the management of multiple customer networks workable. Support is available via chat and they are responsive.
cons: It takes some work with the network devices to get more complex topologies corrected. Sometimes, no matter if SNMP and login is showing as correct the topology will not show accurately. Although chat is responsive for support, most of the time they will have to create a ticket for more complex items and get back with you. Large networks are hard to view i the limited space, an ability to zoom with the a mouse scroll wheel would be really helpful. Also, when searching for a device on the map, it would be nice to have it zoom into that device.

review_title: Auvik in a Helpdesk Environment
description: I am happy to use it everyday and it has quickly become a valuable tool that assists with most problems I am faced with.
author: Joshua D.
pros: I really enjoy the large amount of information that can be parsed through with this tool in a very simplistic and easy way. The reporting is very extensive as well.
cons: The biggest con isn't with the software itself but with the certification. The training and questions do not seem to apply that much to the everyday use of the product that I have observed.

review_title: Very good software driven by a stellar team of devs.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to setup and configure and get visibility into client networks. The product guides you through the setup and implementation in an efficient manner.
cons: Would like better functionality around snmp traps and would like to have the ability to capture network data that is tied to a name within their flow protocol, for example would like To see google.com instead of the IP for a google server.

review_title: Auvik : Outstanding Remote Network Monitoring
description: Auvik has been very good in terms of disaster management and rectifying any kind of infrastructure issue which the team encounters. If there any performance issue due to the network, we can monitor that as well and helps the business with the load tests and we can evaluate the business capability, as per the needs.
author: Nirmesh S.
pros: Auvik is a network remote monitoring and network management tool, which helps poll network 24*7. It uses multiple different protocols as per the availability to poll the devices, which helps to narrow down troubleshooting when you are rectifying any issue in the network infrastructure.
cons: If the network could be monitored via a mobile application like Android and iOS platforms, then it would have been very convenient for the network administrator to take immediate steps if needed. This way the engineers can access the infrastructure anywhere, anytime.

review_title: Great Monitoring System
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This is great because I get alerts when my customers servers go offline. It has really helped with down time when issues arise. Great product!
cons: Honestly, I'm very happy with it and don't see any cons so far. If the occasion arises that I have an issue, I will certainly reach out, but so far, so good.

review_title: Go from 0 to visualized in 20 minutes
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The visualization of the network is about as simple as it gets. The setup is very easy. Not many hours involved in getting to the end goal.
cons: Some basic features are missing, like being able to manually deleted a network element, instead of waiting for it to age off.

review_title: This has made our job as an MSP so much easier
author: Arthur H.
pros: The way it goes out and finds every thing on the network is fantastic. We can on-board a new customer much quicker than we used to. We can quickly hunt down a device that is consuming a lot of bandwidth. The backups of configs is very useful.
cons: It is a little expensive and is sometimes hard to pass this cost onto our customers. We have had instances of the probes slowing down customer networks.

review_title: We have had a great experience using Auvik to monitor and manage our MSP environment.
description: Easily managed infrastructure monitoring and managing of clients. It is cloud based so it requires little of the client's resources to setup and use the product.
author: Jeff H.
pros: The software is easy to setup and get functional monitoring in a very short time. Configuring alerts and management functions makes the product even better to use and recommend.
cons: Sometimes the mapping function behaves a little strange and you need to adjust manually interconnections or the order of uplinks.

review_title: Auvik has made work more productive with its organized interface that is unique compared to others
description: I get to enjoy my job with an organized platform that provides me information for projects and urgent issues that need to be solved right away.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I like most about this software is its ability to show the user visual representations of the data
cons: What I least like about Auvik is that it lacks the ability to view all of its features using a mobile app for apple and android devices.
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Amazing product with an over abundance of features. Excellent service when things go sideways.
author: Matt K.
pros: Incredibly in-depth piece of software that allows reatime tracking of network and infrastructure assets. Amazingly scalable to any infrastructure. Excellent for Root Cause Analysis and network tracing. Fantastic, knowledgeable, friendly support.
cons: Can be cumbersome over slow link WANs (10Mbps point-to-point). Some ConnectWise integration breaks.

review_title: Amazing ability to scale, monitor notify and back up.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use and implement/manage. Auvik is non-platform specific, which makes things nice. Presentation to clients flows in-line with the product.
cons: installing Ubuntu VM for monitoring. This would be simplere if we could use a hosted product instead of using our own or client resources.

review_title: Beyond Belief Network Visibility
description: We never know that we could see so much of our client's networks without the use of a $ Fluke Network device. We are able to see more then out expensive RMM tool is able to provide at a barebones cost. This is a must have for every MSP in their network management tool kit. We are now looking to use it with our Network Audit accounts. The reports are very through and elevate what you are able to document and show your client.
author: Donald H.
pros: How thorough the software is in gathering information on everything that is on the network.
cons: It takes some learning to get the handle on how to properly install the software. The command line entries are one of those points of difficulty that I'm speaking about.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
